title: Evergreen Cannabis Apparel Capsule — Screen-Print Separation & Vector Production Package
one_line_ask: Turn the client's hand-painted hero graphic and brand logo into a production-ready, screen-print-separable vector + halftone package locked to the Evergreen purple/black/pink palette, plus an exported tech-pack mockup, bundled for the decorator.
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the real brief: client "Evergreen" — vertically integrated cannabis retailer in Connecticut/Ohio/Missouri; deliverable = "a production-ready apparel design system for a branded retail capsule" that "Hands cleanly to manufacturers and decorators (vectors, digitized embroidery, tech packs)"; brand is "purple-and-black forward, premium and confident, with slight reference to pink in intentional places"; direction "elevated streetwear"; Fall/Spring 2027 drops; compliant across all three states with no rework. REASONABLY ENRICHED to make a single runnable Adobe-only benchmark: I scoped the engagement down to ONE hero front-print graphic plus the logo lockup (the full multi-piece capsule and embroidery digitizing are out of an image-connector's scope), and I made the concrete deliverable the screen-print SEPARATION + clean vector art + a print-ready mockup export — which is exactly the "vectors... that hands cleanly to manufacturers and decorators" line in the brief, and matches the candidate category screenprint_seps. The specific brand hex values (purple #4B2E83 / black #0B0B0B / accent pink #E8669E), the artwork subject (a Evergreen mountain/lotus crest), and the .ai tech-pack template are invented stand-ins so the chain is executable; no deliverable outside the brief was introduced. The 'worn premium hand-feel' the brand references is produced in-chain by a grain/distress effect on the halftone variant (image_add_grain), not by a supplied stock texture, keeping every asset earned by the connector chain.
full_brief: Evergreen (a CT/OH/MO cannabis retailer) is producing a Fall/Spring 2027 elevated-streetwear capsule sold in-store and via loyalty redemption, and needs the hero front-print graphic handed off to a screen-print decorator in production-ready form. The client supplies two assets: (1) a high-res scan of a hand-painted "Evergreen crest" hero illustration (mountain + lotus motif) shot on textured paper, slightly rotated and with an off-white/warm cast from the scanner; and (2) the Evergreen wordmark logo as a flat bitmap PNG. The contractor must: straighten and tightly crop the scanned hero, correct the warm scanner cast and lift it to clean neutral tone, force the artwork onto the locked brand palette (purple #4B2E83 dominant, black #0B0B0B, intentional accent pink #E8669E only) by collapsing stray colors, isolate the crest from its paper background to a clean transparent subject for separation, convert the cleaned crest to crisp print-ready vector art (the spot-color "separations" a screen shop burns), give a second halftone-screened raster variant of the crest and add a subtle grain/distress pass to it for the worn, premium hand-feel and distressed-overlay look the brand references (Higher Stitch / Supreme direction), vectorize the supplied wordmark to scalable line art, and finally export a genuine authored tech-pack mockup (.ai garment template) showing placement so the decorator sees print size/position. Everything is bundled into one handoff package for the manufacturer. Output must read as premium and confident, on-palette across all three states with no rework, never cartoonish or cheap.

inputs.name: hero_crest_scan.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A high-resolution flatbed-scanner capture of a hand-painted illustration on cream textured watercolor paper: a symmetrical mountain-peak crest with a stylized lotus and small sun motif, painted in deep purple and black gouache with a few stray hand-mixed magenta and teal brush accents. Visible paper grain, brush texture, slight pooling of pigment, a faint warm/yellow scanner color cast, and the artwork sits very slightly rotated (about 3 degrees off-level) with uneven cream margins around it. Documentary scan realism — real paper fiber, no CGI gloss, no perfect edges.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a genuine art scan: warm cast + a few off-palette stray colors + a slight rotation are intentional so the straighten / temperature-correct / palette-collapse steps have real work to do. Single artwork centered on paper, no surrounding clutter.

inputs.name: evergreen_la_wordmark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat bitmap PNG of the wordmark 'Evergreen' set in a confident high-contrast condensed serif, solid deep-purple letterforms on a plain white background, clean even kerning, centered, no effects or gradients — a logo handoff bitmap as a client would email it. Crisp but rasterized (slightly soft pixel edges, not vector).
inputs.realism_notes: Text-bearing asset → gemini-3-pro-image for accurate legible lettering. Deliberately a flat raster (not already vector) so the vectorize step is justified.

inputs.name: evergreen_la_techpack_template.ai
inputs.gen_prompt: An authored Adobe Illustrator (.ai) garment tech-pack template: a flat front view of a premium heavyweight crewneck tee on a neutral artboard, with a labelled print-placement box (centered chest, ~11in wide), spec callouts for print location and max print size, and a brand color-callout strip referencing purple #4B2E83 / black #0B0B0B / pink #E8669E. Genuine .ai with real artboard and placed art, ready to export.
inputs.realism_notes: This is a CLIENT/STUDIO-AUTHORED .ai (not composed by the connector). document_render_vector only EXPORTS it — listing it as an authored input keeps the workflow honest (no layout composition by the connector).

outputs.name: crest_vector_seps.svg
outputs.spec: Clean print-ready vector of the hero crest (transparent), spot-color-friendly flat shapes locked to #4B2E83 / #0B0B0B / #E8669E — the screen-print separation art the decorator burns.

outputs.name: crest_halftone_distress.png
outputs.spec: Halftone/duotone-screened raster variant of the crest (purple/black) for the distressed-overlay look, transparent PNG, print resolution.

outputs.name: wordmark_vector.svg
outputs.spec: Vectorized Evergreen wordmark, scalable solid-purple line art for embroidery/print handoff.

outputs.name: evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf
outputs.spec: Exported tech-pack/placement mockup from the authored .ai garment template, showing chest print placement and color callouts.

outputs.name: Evergreen_Capsule_PrintPackage.zip
outputs.spec: Single decorator handoff bundle containing the two vectors, the halftone PNG, the cleaned transparent crest, and the tech-pack PDF.

connector_workflow.note: Initialize upload of the client's hand-painted hero scan (hero_crest_scan.png) into CC storage.
Finalize the upload → addressable CC asset URI for the hero scan. This is the only raw asset-load step; everything downstream chains from it.
Auto-straighten the ~3-degree-rotated scan so the crest sits level for cropping and vectorization.
Crop off the uneven cream paper margins, tight to the artwork bounds.
Cool the warm/yellow scanner cast back to neutral so brand purples read true, not muddy.
Auto-tone to clean, even contrast — lifts the off-white toward a printable clean base before palette enforcement.
Select the stray off-palette accents (the hand-mixed magenta/teal brush marks) by prompt so they can be collapsed off the locked palette.
Solid-fill the selected stray-color region with brand purple #4B2E83, forcing the artwork onto the locked palette (image_fill_area = solid color only, used correctly here).
Knock out the paper background → clean transparent crest subject, the prerequisite for clean separations.
Vectorize the transparent crest into crisp flat spot-color shapes → the screen-print separation art (crest_vector_seps.svg). DELIVERABLE 1.
Branch from the same transparent crest: apply a purple/black halftone screen for the distressed-overlay print variant. Intermediate — a grain/distress pass follows.

Add a subtle grain/distress pass to the halftone variant for the worn, premium hand-feel the brand references (crest_halftone_distress.png). DELIVERABLE 2. This in-chain effect replaces any supplied stock paper texture.
Initialize upload of the client wordmark bitmap (evergreen_la_wordmark.png).
Finalize wordmark upload → CC asset URI.
Vectorize the rasterized wordmark into scalable solid-purple line art (wordmark_vector.svg) for print/embroidery handoff. DELIVERABLE 3.
Initialize upload of the authored garment tech-pack template (evergreen_la_techpack_template.ai).
Finalize the authored .ai upload → CC asset URI. (Authored template input; the connector only EXPORTS it, does not compose layout.)
Export the authored .ai tech-pack template → placement-mockup PDF showing chest print position + color callouts (evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf). DELIVERABLE 4.
Create the decorator handoff folder in CC storage to collect all final deliverables.
Copy all final outputs (two vectors, halftone PNG, cleaned transparent crest, tech-pack PDF) into the handoff folder.
Single unavoidable local step: zip the collected handoff folder into one manufacturer package (Evergreen_Capsule_PrintPackage.zip). DELIVERABLE 5.

chaining_note: Single backbone with two deliberate branches, all output→input chained. The only raw asset loads are the three uploads (steps 1-2 hero, 13-14 wordmark, 16-17 tech-pack .ai); every processing step consumes a prior output. Hero pipeline: hero_asset → auto_straighten → crop_to_bounds → adjust_color_temperature → apply_auto_tone → (select_by_prompt produces stray_color_mask, consumed with hero_toned by fill_area) → hero_onpalette → remove_background → crest_transparent. crest_transparent then FORKS: vectorize → crest_vector_seps.svg (separations) AND apply_halftone → crest_halftone (intermediate) → add_grain → crest_halftone_distress.png (worn/distress variant; the grain pass supplies the 'premium hand-feel' in-chain, no external stock texture). Wordmark branch: wordmark_asset → vectorize → wordmark_vector.svg. Tech-pack branch: authored techpack_ai_asset → document_render_vector (export only) → mockup.pdf. Convergence: asset_create_folders → asset_copy_assets gathers crest_vector_seps + crest_halftone_distress + wordmark_vector + crest_transparent + techpack mockup into handoff_collected, and the single local [L] zip step bundles them into the final package. No step floats free; the deliverables literally emerge from the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon (21 steps, 15 distinct Adobe tools across A1 photo/raster, A2 document export, and A4 asset/storage groups), well past the ≥10 calls / ≥6 distinct-tool bar. Difficulty comes from honest sequencing: straighten/crop/temperature/auto-tone must precede palette work; the select_by_prompt → fill_area mask handoff enforces brand-palette discipline using fill_area in its ONLY legal mode (solid color, not generative); remove_background must precede vectorize for clean separations; and the chain forks correctly so one cleaned crest feeds both the vector seps and the halftone-then-grain distress variant (halftone → add_grain produces the worn hand-feel in-chain, no external stock texture). The tech-pack uses an AUTHORED .ai exported via document_render_vector (no layout composition by the connector). Every step is [C] except a single unavoidable [L] zip — no [W]/[A]/[T] dependencies, no not-available capabilities (no gen-fill, no AI object removal, no upscaling, no compositing, no bg-replace-by-prompt). Faithful to the brief's explicit 'vectors that hand cleanly to manufacturers and decorators' deliverable for the screenprint_seps category.

issues: No hard-constraint violations. All 15 distinct Adobe tools are present and [C]-tagged in CONNECTOR_CAPABILITIES_v2.md (A1 raster, A2 document, A4 asset groups).
pure_adobe PASS: steps 1-20 all server==adobe; only step 21 (local_zip_package) is [L], a single tolerated trailing packaging step — no core work is local.
no_canva PASS: no Canva tools. no_express_widget PASS: no search_design/fill_text/animate_design/change_background_color.

chained PASS: 17 of 20 post-first steps consume a prior output (~85% >= 80%). The 3 non-chaining steps (13, 16, 19) are legitimate fresh asset-upload inits / folder create, exactly as the chaining_note discloses.

feasible_no_X PASS: image_fill_area (step 8) used in its ONLY legal mode — solid custom-RGB fill of a select_by_prompt mask, explicitly not generative; the spec note flags this correctly. document_render_vector (step 18) exports a GENUINE authored .ai (evergreen_la_techpack_template.ai listed as a vector input) and only EXPORTS — no layout composition by the connector. No gen-fill, AI object-removal, text-to-image, compositing, bg-replace-by-prompt, or upscaling anywhere.

RESOLVED (orphaned input): the former grunge_paper_texture.jpg asset — listed in inputs[] but consumed by no step — has been removed, and the 'worn premium hand-feel' it stood in for is now produced in-chain by image_add_grain (step 12) applied to the halftone variant. Every listed input asset is now consumed by the workflow.

MINOR (no fix required): image_apply_halftone (step 11) is described in the sheet as duotone/print-screen; the spec's 'purple/black halftone' variant stays within that documented duotone purpose but the exact spot-color mapping is the softest claim in the chain — still not an [X] capability.

MINOR (no fix required): step 20 copies crest_transparent (the step-9 intermediate) into the handoff bundle; it is a valid addressable CC asset so copying it is legal, though it is an intermediate rather than a dedicated export step.

Counts verified: tool_call_count=21 (20 Adobe + 1 local), distinct_adobe_tools=15; both exceed the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool bar. Long-horizon flagship qualifying (>=12 calls across >=3 groups, output->input chained).
